Title: Global hypermethylation of the N6-methyladenosine RNA modification associated with apple heterografting

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Golden delicious / Malus prunifolia ‘Fupingqiuzi’ grafting system
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf tissues of grafted scion/rootstock context
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Abiotic stress resistance, especially drought/water deprivation response
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with enhanced ABA transport/homeostasis and improved stress resistance in heterografted apple; hyper-m6A and higher transcript abundance in both scion and rootstock comparisons
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABCG22 was identified as a mobile mRNA with differential m6A modification and expression in heterografted apples. 2. In hgGd vs sgGd, MdABCG22 showed increased m6A enrichment and increased transcript level. 3. In hgMp vs sgMp, MdABCG22 also showed increased m6A enrichment and increased transcript level. 4. The gene was highlighted as a positive drought-response regulator and proposed to contribute to better heterograft performance through ABA transport/homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ABCG22 was also among genes overlapping m6A change, DNA methylation change, mobility, and expression change; authors propose cooperative regulation by m6A and 5mC.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in heterografted scion and rootstock relative to self-grafted controls
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf samples from mature leaves collected from grafted plants
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mobile mRNA identification by SNP-based analysis; m6A-IP-qPCR; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heterografting versus self-grafting; water deprivation/drought-related functional interpretation
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Hypermethylated at m6A level in heterografts; also associated with DNA methylation changes in hgGd vs sgGd
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate marker/gene for improving drought tolerance in grafted apple via rootstock-scion combinations and epitranscriptomic regulation
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: MeRIP-seq PRJNA793081; RNA-seq PRJNA793080; BS-seq PRJNA793082; sRNA-seq PRJNA793083

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Abiotic stress tolerance, especially salt and osmotic stress
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mobile mRNA with increased m6A enrichment and increased transcript level in heterografted scion, associated with stress resistance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdBZIP17 was among rootstock-to-scion mobile mRNAs with hyperm6A and upregulation in hgGd vs sgGd. 2. Authors linked it to salt and osmotic stress responses. 3. Increased m6A enrichment and expression were validated.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although a TF by family, in this paper it was not the principal mechanistic regulatory focus for metabolite control; retained as common stress-related core mobile gene in nutrient/stress metabolism context.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in heterografted scion relative to self-grafted scion
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: m6A-IP-qPCR; RT-qPCR; mobile mRNA SNP-based identification
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heterografting; salt and osmotic stress annotation

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mobile mRNA from rootstock to scion with increased m6A enrichment and higher expression in heterografted scion, associated with improved defense-related performance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdSDR1 was identified as a rootstock-to-scion mobile mRNA with hyperm6A and upregulation in hgGd vs sgGd. 2. Its mobility was directly validated by dCAPS/HpaII assay. 3. Increased m6A enrichment and transcript abundance were validated by m6A-IP-qPCR and RT-qPCR.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a representative mobile transcript validation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in heterografted scion relative to self-grafted scion
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SNP-based mobility analysis; dCAPS assay; m6A-IP-qPCR; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for graft-transmitted defense traits

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: U2 auxiliary factor small subunit/splicing factor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mobile mRNA showing hypom6A but increased transcript abundance in heterografted scion and rootstock comparisons
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdU2AF35B was identified as a mobile mRNA with reduced m6A enrichment and increased expression in heterografted tissues. 2. This opposite relationship was validated in both scion and rootstock validation sets. 3. Functional annotation related to photoperiodism, flowering, and mRNA splicing rather than direct nutrient metabolism.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally validated among mobile mRNAs, but nutrient-metabolism relevance is indirect.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in heterografted scion and rootstock relative to self-grafted controls
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: m6A-IP-qPCR; RT-qPCR; mobile mRNA SNP-based identification

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: m6A RNA modification / indirect effects on ABA-related genes
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Epitranscriptomic regulation linked to stress-response and DNA methylation pathways
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation of MdMTA induces global m6A hypomethylation and activates DNA demethylase genes, linking m6A to 5mC regulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMTA is the apple m6A methyltransferase ortholog of METTL3. 2. MdMTA RNAi lines showed altered expression of DNA methylation-related genes, including strong activation of MdROS1.1, MdROS1.2, and MdDME1.1. 3. Authors propose MdMTA as a mediator connecting m6A deposition with DNA methylation status during grafting.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central epitranscriptomic regulator, but not a metabolite-pathway gene; effect on nutrient metabolism is indirect via transcriptome/epigenome remodeling and ABA-related genes.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated expression discussed as affecting global m6A levels
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi transgenic analysis; transcript profiling of DNA methylation-related genes
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Catalyzes m6A deposition; its reduction causes transcriptome-wide hypomethylation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering entry point to manipulate graft-associated epitranscriptomic and stress traits, with caution due to global effects
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Global epitranscriptomic perturbation may cause broad pleiotropic effects
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: MeRIP-seq PRJNA793081; RNA-seq PRJNA793080; BS-seq PRJNA793082

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Epigenetic regulation affecting stress- and development-related gene expression
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is activated when MdMTA is downregulated, supporting crosstalk between m6A and DNA methylation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdROS1 family genes were upregulated in MdMTA RNAi plants. 2. Authors infer that reduced m6A methylation promotes DNA demethylase expression. 3. This supports a model of coordinated m6A and 5mC regulation in apple grafting.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported as MdROS1.1 and MdROS1.2 in figure text; abstract highlights MdROS1.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in MdMTA RNAi transgenic plants
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis in MdMTA RNAi lines
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential epigenetic target but likely pleiotropic
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad DNA methylation changes likely pleiotropic

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Carotenoid cleavage dioxygenase family, non-heme iron dioxygenase
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Apocarotenoid products contributing to ABA biosynthesis context
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative cleavage of carotenoid substrates to generate apocarotenoids in the ABA synthesis-related context described by the paper
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported by authors as a key enzyme of ABA synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf tissues in grafted scion/rootstock context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Water deprivation/drought response and stress resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA synthesis-related stress resistance; mobile mRNA is hyperm6A and upregulated in both heterografted scion and rootstock comparisons
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdCCD1 was identified as a bidirectionally mobile mRNA associated with heterografting. 2. In both hgGd vs sgGd and hgMp vs sgMp, MdCCD1 showed increased m6A enrichment and increased transcript abundance. 3. m6A-IP-qPCR and RT-qPCR validated the higher m6A and expression. 4. Authors highlighted CCD1 as a key enzyme of ABA synthesis and a positive drought regulator contributing to improved heterograft performance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A central metabolism-related causal gene emphasized in abstract, results, discussion, and model figure.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in heterografted scion and rootstock relative to self-grafted controls
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SNP-based mobile mRNA identification; m6A-IP-qPCR; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heterografting versus self-grafting; drought/water deprivation functional interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Hyperm6A in heterografted scion and rootstock
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for graft-based or transgenic improvement of drought/stress resistance through ABA-related metabolism; promising for stacking with ABA transport genes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: MeRIP-seq PRJNA793081; RNA-seq PRJNA793080; sRNA-seq PRJNA793083; BS-seq PRJNA793082

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mobile mRNA with increased m6A enrichment and increased expression in heterografted scion, associated with defense/stress-related metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdGAD4 was a rootstock-to-scion mobile mRNA with hyperm6A and upregulation in hgGd vs sgGd. 2. Increased m6A and transcript abundance were validated. 3. The gene was linked to defense response and flower development.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not emphasized in final model, but experimentally validated and metabolism-related.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible auxiliary target for improving stress resilience via amino acid metabolism

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes beta-glucosidic bonds of glucosylated metabolites
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for remodeling of metabolite pools
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mobile mRNA with increased m6A enrichment and expression in heterografted rootstock, associated with stress-related metabolic remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdBGLU11 was identified among scion-to-rootstock mobile mRNAs with hyperm6A and upregulation in hgMp vs sgMp. 2. m6A and transcript increases were validated. 3. Functional annotation linked it to inorganic substance response and seed development.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Metabolic target is broad and not precisely resolved in paper.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for stress-associated metabolic responses

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves HMG-CoA to acetyl-CoA and acetoacetate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mobile mRNA with increased m6A enrichment and increased expression in heterografted rootstock, associated with stress-related metabolic performance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdHGML was identified among scion-to-rootstock mobile mRNAs with hyperm6A and upregulation in hgMp vs sgMp. 2. Validation confirmed increased m6A enrichment and transcript abundance. 3. The gene was linked to responses to wounding, inorganic substances, jasmonic acid, and light stimulus.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as stress-associated rather than specific nutrient-quality determinant.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible auxiliary target for stress-related metabolic engineering
